question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who controls the network path between the pinger and the mirror REST endpoint (HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST) serve a crafted HTTP response from buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() that injects attacker-controlled gRPC node endpoints, causing all subsequent NewTransferTransaction().Execute(client) calls to route signed HBAR transfers to attacker-owned consensus nodes, resulting in direct loss of operator funds?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external attacker perform a DNS poisoning attack against the domain names returned in serviceEndpoint.DomainName fields, causing net.JoinHostPort(host, port) to resolve to an attacker-controlled IP, so that all cryptoTransfer.Execute(client) calls send real HBAR to a malicious node that pockets the funds?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external attacker exploit the fact that amountTinybar is only validated for == 0 (not for negative values) by injecting a negative HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_AMOUNT_TINYBAR environment variable, causing AddHbarTransfer(client.GetOperatorAccountID(), HbarFromTinybar(-cfg.amountTinybar)) to add a positive debit to the operator and AddHbarTransfer(toID, HbarFromTinybar(cfg.amountTinybar)) to credit a negative amount, reversing the transfer direction and draining the operator account?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who can delay or drop the GetReceipt(client) response cause the retry loop to re-execute cryptoTransfer.Execute(client) up to maxRetries+1 times for a single tick, resulting in cfg.amountTinybar * (maxRetries+1) tinybar being transferred out of the operator account per interval tick?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external attacker exploit the absence of an upper bound on HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_AMOUNT_TINYBAR (only checked for != 0) to set amountTinybar to math.MaxInt64, causing HbarFromTinybar(cfg.amountTinybar) to overflow or transfer the entire operator balance in a single Execute(client) call?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who controls a rogue mirror REST server (reachable via HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST=http://attacker.com) return a nodesEnvelope JSON payload with attacker-controlled NodeAccountID and ServiceEndpoints, causing newClient() to build a network map pointing entirely to attacker nodes, so every subsequent HBAR transfer is submitted to and confirmed by attacker-controlled infrastructure?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external attacker exploit the hardcoded default operatorKey (302e020100300506032b65700422042091132178e72057a1d7528025956fe39b0b847f200ab59b2fdd367017f3087137) embedded in the flag default to sign and submit unauthorized HBAR transfers from the default operator account (0.0.2) if the production deployment accidentally uses the default key?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who controls the gRPC response from a consensus node cause Execute(client) to return a nil error while the transaction is actually rejected on-chain, then cause GetReceipt(client) to return an error, triggering a retry that submits a second identical transfer, resulting in double-spend of cfg.amountTinybar from the operator account?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external attacker perform an HTTP response injection (via a man-in-the-middle on the plain http:// mirror REST URL) to replace the nodes list with a single entry pointing to an attacker-controlled endpoint, so that the hiero.Client built by newClient() exclusively routes all signed transfer transactions to the attacker?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external attacker exploit the absence of a maximum bound on HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MAX_RETRIES to set maxRetries to a very large integer (e.g., 2^31-1), causing submitWithRetry() to loop indefinitely and submit cfg.amountTinybar tinybar transfers continuously until the operator account is fully drained?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external attacker exploit the absence of a minimum bound on HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_INTERVAL (only checked for > 0) by setting interval to 1 nanosecond, causing the time.NewTicker(cfg.interval) to fire at maximum CPU rate and submitWithRetry() to be called millions of times per second, draining the operator account through rapid repeated HBAR transfers?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external attacker inject a crafted serviceEndpoint with an IPAddressV4 field containing a private/internal IP (e.g., 169.254.169.254 or 10.0.0.1) to redirect HBAR transfer transactions to an internal network node that the attacker controls, causing funds to be sent to an unintended destination?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external attacker set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_TO_ACCOUNT_ID to an attacker-controlled Hedera account ID (e.g., 0.0.99999), causing all periodic AddHbarTransfer(toID, HbarFromTinybar(cfg.amountTinybar)) calls to credit the attacker's account with cfg.amountTinybar tinybar every cfg.interval seconds?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: backoff()] Can an unprivileged external attacker trigger integer overflow in backoff() by causing attempt values where 1<<(attempt-1) overflows int, resulting in a negative or zero sleep duration that causes the retry loop to spin without delay and submit excessive HBAR transfers from the operator account?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who controls the consensus node's gRPC response manipulate the receipt status returned by GetReceipt(client) to always return an error, causing the retry loop to exhaust all maxRetries+1 attempts and submit cfg.amountTinybar * (maxRetries+1) tinybar in total per tick, multiplying fund loss?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external attacker exploit the absence of TLS certificate validation on the plain http:// mirror REST URL (HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST) to perform a TLS stripping or HTTP interception attack, injecting a malicious nodesEnvelope response that redirects all HBAR transfers to attacker-controlled nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external attacker return a nodesEnvelope with a nodeEntry where NodeAccountID is a valid Hedera account ID owned by the attacker, causing the hiero.Client network map to associate attacker-controlled endpoints with a legitimate-looking account ID, so that HBAR transfers are routed to and accepted by the attacker's node?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external attacker set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_AMOUNT_TINYBAR to math.MinInt64 (-9223372036854775808), causing HbarFromTinybar(-cfg.amountTinybar) to overflow to math.MinInt64 (since -MinInt64 == MinInt64 in two's complement), resulting in an unintended massive debit from the operator account?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who can delay gRPC responses beyond the SDK's internal timeout cause Execute(client) to return a timeout error, triggering a retry that re-submits the same transfer transaction with a new transaction ID, resulting in both the original and retry transactions being confirmed on-chain and double-charging the operator?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external attacker exploit the absence of response size limits in json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&payload) to return a massive nodesEnvelope JSON payload that causes memory exhaustion, crashing the pinger and preventing it from detecting or stopping runaway transfer loops?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external attacker inject a serviceEndpoint with Port=0 for all legitimate nodes but Port=50211 for an attacker-controlled host, causing the network map to contain only the attacker's endpoint (since entries with Port==0 are skipped), routing all HBAR transfers exclusively to the attacker?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external attacker exploit the envOr() function's behavior of accepting any non-empty, non-whitespace string for HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_KEY to inject a known private key, allowing the attacker to independently sign and submit transactions from the operator account outside of the pinger, draining funds?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who controls the mirror node REST endpoint return an empty nodes list (len(network) == 0) after the client is initialized, causing newClient() to fail and the pinger to crash, but not before a partial set of transfers has already been submitted and confirmed on-chain?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external attacker exploit the fact that submitWithRetry() is called synchronously in the ticker loop without any concurrency guard, so that if a tick fires while a previous submitWithRetry() is still executing (due to slow gRPC), two goroutines could simultaneously call Execute(client) and submit duplicate HBAR transfers?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external attacker exploit the absence of HTTPS enforcement on the mirror REST URL to perform an ARP spoofing or BGP hijacking attack on the network path, intercepting the GET /api/v1/network/nodes response and injecting attacker-controlled node endpoints before the pinger builds its hiero.Client network map?
